questions.q: Which of the following best describes the purpose of the Opportunity Score in Ads Manager?
options: Predict the expected ROAS of the next campaign
Show how optimized your campaigns are based on experimentally proven recommendations not yet applied
Measure the quality of the creative against industry benchmarks
Estimate the cost per result for next quarter
questions.explanation: The Opportunity Score is a 0-100 score plus real recommendations. It does NOT predict or guarantee performance — it reflects the adoption of proven best practices.

questions.q: At which campaign creation level do you make the selections for audience, conversion location, and placements?
questions.explanation: Audience, conversion settings, and placements are ad set level decisions. Campaign decides buying type/objective/budget. Ad decides creative/destination/tracking.

questions.q: A client wants to automate their ENTIRE campaign using AI to sell products on their website. What do you recommend?
questions.explanation: Advantage+ Sales campaigns is a 'complete campaign' solution that automates the entire campaign. The rest are single-step (they automate a single aspect).

questions.q: Which of these are single-step Advantage+ solutions? Select 2.
questions.explanation: Placements and audience are single-step. Sales and Leads campaigns are complete campaign solutions.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT one of Meta's 7 official Performance Best Practices?
questions.explanation: The 7 BPs are: Automate setup, Align objectives, Consider audience, Optimize creative & placements, Focus on signals, Get the most out of budget & bidding, Validate results. Frequency caps is NOT on the list.

questions.q: Which Best Practice corresponds to making decisions with high-quality data (Pixel, CAPI, datasets)?
questions.explanation: BP 5 Focus on signals: 'Inform decision making and optimize your strategy with high-quality data.' Signals = data sources.

questions.q: In a Business Portfolio, which option correctly describes the 3 types of users?
options: People (individuals), Partners (other businesses with their own portfolio), System users (servers/software with API access)
questions.explanation: People = individual people. Partners = other businesses (they need their own portfolio + business ID). System users = servers/software with API access. Not all portfolios have system users available.

questions.q: Temporary access in a Business Portfolio lasts:
questions.explanation: Temporary access: minimum 3 days, maximum 75 days. It is an advanced option to grant time-limited basic access.

questions.q: What is the simplest way to exclude groups of sensitive content in the Brand Safety Center?
options: Exclude topics such as Gaming, News, and Politics
Set up content type exclusions for live video
questions.explanation: Inventory filters (Expanded/Moderate/Limited) are the simplest control with the broadest coverage. Block lists require manual work; topic exclusions only apply to FB in-stream/reels.

questions.q: What access level or role is required to change the payment methods connected to Meta Business Suite?
options: Admin OR finance role (view and manage)
Only the original creator of the portfolio
questions.explanation: Changing payment methods requires admin access OR the finance role (view and manage). The finance role is one of the portfolio's Advanced options.

questions.q: An account manager is given employee access within Business Manager. What task can that user perform?
options: Delete the business from Business Manager
Access only the accounts and tools assigned to them
questions.explanation: Employee access = limited access: the user can only work with the accounts and assets an admin assigns to them. Deleting the business, managing other employees, or creating accounts/Pages are tasks exclusive to admin access.

questions.q: A company used Advantage+ placements and got slightly better results on Instagram. What should the media buyer do in the next campaign?
options: Place the ads only on Instagram from now on
Keep using the same strategy with Advantage+ placements
A/B test Instagram feed vs Instagram stories
questions.explanation: A slight difference does not justify breaking Advantage+ placements: the system already distributes delivery to the most efficient placement in real time. Limiting to a single placement or fragmenting by placement reduces the optimization pool and usually worsens the cost per result.
